findings.description: The spikerush distribution map (Figure 7) in the HTML version shows dots in the southwestern portion of the lake, whereas the PDF version shows dots in the northwestern portion of the lake.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 7 shows spikerush dots clustered in the northwest/north-central area. HTML Figure 7 shows spikerush dots clustered in the southwest/south-central area, suggesting the image may be different or the map orientation differs.

findings.description: Table 1 and Table 2 in the HTML use colored highlighting (red/green) on the 2021 column values, which is not present in the PDF version.
findings.evidence: PDF Tables 1 and 2 show plain text for all cells. HTML Tables 1 and 2 show red and green background highlighting on 2021 column values.

findings.description: The HTML has two nested <main> elements, and the document title is split across h1 ('2021 White Lake') and h2 ('Aquatic Vegetation Survey'), which is acceptable but the heading hierarchy has some issues. 'Whole Lake Survey Results' is an h2 which is correct, but 'References:' and table titles use h3, while the PDF treats them as bold section headers at the same level.
findings.evidence: HTML uses h1 for '2021 White Lake', h2 for 'Aquatic Vegetation Survey' and 'Whole Lake Survey Results', and h3 for 'References:', 'Table 1:', 'Table 2:'. This is a reasonable structural choice.

findings.description:
Some figure labels contain stray text artifacts ('A', '2020', '2021') that appear as standalone

elements before or after the images, duplicating label text already in the figcaptions.
findings.evidence:
Figure 4 has '

A

' and '

2021

' outside the figcaption. Figure 5 has '

A

'. Figure 10 has '

A

'. Figure 11 has '

2020

'. These are artifacts from the PDF page layout but appear as duplicated/misplaced content.

findings.description: Minor text variation: the PDF text says 'Myriophyllum tentellum' in the species list on page 3, while the HTML reproduces this same text. However, elsewhere the PDF uses 'Myriophyllum tenellum' (one 't'). The HTML faithfully reproduces both spellings as found in the PDF, so this is a PDF-internal inconsistency, not an HTML error.
findings.evidence: Page 3 PDF: 'Myriophyllum tentellum'; Page 4 PDF: 'Myriophyllum tenellum'. HTML reproduces both.

findings.description: The URL from the References section is present in the HTML as plain text but is not rendered as a hyperlink, whereas the PDF shows it as a clickable link.
findings.evidence: PDF has hyperlinked 'https://auth1.dpr.ncparks.gov/flora/species_account.php?id=4052'. HTML includes it as plain text within the paragraph.

findings.description: Figure 10 has a second embedded image with alt text 'Decorative graphic or legend label' which appears to be a small artifact image from the conversion. The alt text is a guess and may not accurately describe the original content.
